prompt:
dramatic side-view photograph of ancient carved rice terraces on a steep 
mountainside at dawn, each individual terrace-step is clearly sloping 
downward left-to-right with water visibly pooling at the lower edge of 
each step, but the entire mountain face rises sharply from left to right, 
both the local downward slope of every terrace and the global upward 
ascent of the mountain are simultaneously undeniable in one frame, thin 
morning mist threads between the terrace levels separating each one as a 
distinct unit, golden raking light from the left catches the water on 
each terrace and the ascending ridge line, photorealistic, shot on 
Hasselblad medium format, 80mm lens, f/8, deep focus throughout, 
Kodachrome palette, no figures, no text, no symbols, no annotations
--ar 16:9 --style raw --v 6.1
visual_logic: Terraced rice paddies where every individual terrace slopes downward right but the mountain ascends left-to-right; local and global directionality are simultaneously opposite and both visually undeniable.
